status: P1 EVIDENCE CLOSURE WAVE 7 / SEQUENCE OR SUPPLIER-EQUIVALENCE BLOCK ACTIVE / NOT PLATINUM CERTIFIED
classification: Supplier-named bronchial / respiratory short regulatory peptide family
mechanism_summary: No canonical mechanism is assigned. The product name suggests an organ-targeted commercial positioning, but exact sequence, target and pharmacology require direct primary-source and analytical closure.
evidence_maturity: Regional short-peptide, cell, animal or limited human literature may exist under this name or related names. Evidence is not attributed to the catalogue product until chemical identity and source linkage are confirmed.
regulatory_headline: IDENTITY-SENSITIVE SUPPLIER PRODUCT FAMILY / NO UNIVERSAL APPROVAL OR ORGAN-TREATMENT CLAIM
certification_blockers: Molecular formula, mass and reference standard
Current product- and jurisdiction-specific regulatory status
closure_class: AEDL TETRAPEPTIDE / RESPIRATORY POSITIONING AND HUMAN EVIDENCE
closure_state: PARTIAL CLOSURE — BRONCHOGEN LITERATURE IDENTITY ALA-GLU-ASP-LEU (AEDL) AND CELLULAR PROGRAMME IDENTIFIED; BR20 TERMINI, SALT, ANALYTICAL IDENTITY, STERILITY AND HUMAN BENEFIT OPEN
authoritative_product_boundary: Primary publications identify Bronchogen as Ala-Glu-Asp-Leu (AEDL) and report DNA-binding, plant-callus and aged bronchial-cell differentiation experiments. These findings do not establish treatment of respiratory disease or analytical equivalence of BR20.
critical_closure_controls: AEDL identity is literature identity, not BR20 proof
Bronchial-cell and plant-callus studies are not clinical respiratory evidence
No asthma, bronchitis, lung-repair or pulmonary-ageing claim
No organ-targeting specificity claim without human evidence
No dosing, injection or reconstitution protocol
